commands.review_summary:
The PR updates iOS onboarding gateway connection flows, Tailscale/manual connection handling, persisted last-connection metadata, auth issue classification, and related Swift tests.

PR surface: Other +813. Total +813 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. for the PR's main value: it adds a new iOS onboarding connection chooser and explicit Tailscale/raw-IP path. Source inspection is enough to see the changed reconnect boundary, but I did not run a live iOS flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This protected-label PR needs maintainer/security policy review and real behavior proof, not an autonomous repair lane.

target.title: feat(ios): improve onboarding gateway connection flow
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

iOS onboarding made gateway connection feel brittle: users had to choose between QR/setup code, LAN discovery, and remote/Tailscale concepts without a clear first-run path. Raw Tailscale IP entry also needed to work for a real tailnet gateway without weakening generic QR/deep-link security.

## Why This Change Was Made

This replaces the first onboarding screen with a connection-path chooser for QR/setup code, local auto-find, Tailscale/remote, and manual setup. It adds a first-class Tailscale entry path with MagicDNS/Serve and raw 100.64/10 IP handling, but scopes plaintext raw-IP use to the explicit Tailscale path after a tailnet-style interface is present. Generic QR/setup-code/deep-link parsing still rejects plaintext CGNAT setup codes.

It also improves auth recovery copy by distinguishing token-missing, password-missing, and generic unauthorized gateway states, and persists the Tailscale plaintext route context so follow-up retry/reconnect does not silently switch the same raw IP to TLS.

## User Impact

New users get a clearer onboarding decision tree and a smoother path for already-on-tailnet devices: paste a MagicDNS name, Tailscale Serve URL, or raw 100.x gateway IP, then connect. If Tailscale is off, the app now tells the user to turn it on before retrying instead of failing with a vague gateway error.

The QR/setup-code auth contract remains unchanged: raw gateway token/password are not added to QR output by this PR.

## Evidence

- `swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter DeepLinksSecurityTests`
- `xcodebuild -quiet test -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClawLogicTests -configuration Debug -destination 'id=056A94B1-69E3-49EA-BA1D-1A0F58D36436' -derivedDataPath apps/ios/build/CodexOnboardingP2LogicTests`
- `xcodebuild -quiet build -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClaw -configuration Debug -destination 'id=056A94B1-69E3-49EA-BA1D-1A0F58D36436' -derivedDataPath apps/ios/build/CodexOnboardingP2Sim`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings

Known existing build warnings remain in unrelated files: `NodeAppModel.swift`, `TalkModeManager.swift`, and `OpenClawChatUI/ChatViewModel.swift`.
target.author: solvely-colin
